Dear John:
This letter is confirming our telephone conversation on
the question whether a city Council nember uay use carnpaign
funds to purchase a ticket to another candidate's campaign
dinner.
when the assurnptions was nade that the candidate is
running for other than a legislative office there lras neither
a restriction on the purchase nor on the amount of the
purchase. This renains true so long as the purchase is not
for personal use. A council menber may use carnpaign funds to
purchase such a ticket so long as a political purpose of the
council member is served.
In the event it is a legislative candidate the sane
analysis would apply so long as the cost of the ticket is
less than one thousand dollars ($1,000.00).
Please advise me if this infornation is incorrect. If
we do not hear fron you lre will provide this information to
our city Council .
Thank you again for your assistance.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city council member may use campaign funds to purchase a ticket to another candidate's campaign dinner, and if so, what restrictions apply based on the candidate's office.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A council member may use campaign funds to purchase a ticket to another candidate's campaign dinner if a political purpose is served and it is not for personal use. For non-legislative candidates, there is no limit on the purchase amount. For legislative candidates, the ticket cost must be less than $1,000.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming correspondence from a City Attorney to FPPC, reporting back on a prior telephone conversation with FPPC staff. It references FPPC advice but is not itself an official FPPC advice letter. The document appears to be the City Attorney's confirmation/documentation of FPPC guidance received. OCR quality issues present (e.g., 'Et)' at top, spacing inconsistencies).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a city council member may use campaign funds to purchase a ticket to another candidate's campaign dinner, and if so, what restrictions apply based on the candidate's office.

CONCLUSION: A council member may use campaign funds to purchase a ticket to another candidate's campaign dinner if a political purpose is served and it is not for personal use. For non-legislative candidates, there is no limit on the purchase amount. For legislative candidates, the ticket cost must be less than $1,000.

This renains true so long as the purchase is not for personal use. A council menber may use carnpaign funds to purchase such a ticket so long as a political purpose of the council member is served. In the event it is a legislative candidate the sane analysis would apply so long as the cost of the ticket is less than one thousand dollars ($1,000.00). Please advise me if this infornation is incorrect. If we do not hear fron you lre will provide this information to our city Council . Thank you again for your assistance. nv-4 : 89 Deputy city Attorney 3031 Torrance Boul*"rd ' Torru" Elizabeth(6. Clark
embedding.summary: City Attorney of Torrance confirms with FPPC that a city council member may use campaign funds to purchase tickets to another candidate's campaign dinner, subject to restrictions based on the candidate's office and the absence of personal use.
